2606.16152.title: The Quality-Utility Paradox: Why High-Reward Data Impairs Small Model Mathematical Reasoning
2606.16152.paper_summary: Knowledge distillation from powerful reasoning models is widely used to improve Small Language Models (SLMs) on mathematical reasoning, often assuming that traces with higher reward model scores provide more useful supervision. We identify a counterintuitive \textbf{Quality-Utility Paradox} in mathematical reasoning distillation. Data refined or synthesized by a stronger Oracle obtains higher perceived quality according to reward models, yet consistently underperforms traces generated by the SLM itself and selected through rejection sampling across Qwen2.5, LLaMA-3, and DeepSeek families. Our analysis shows that Oracle refinement couples logical repair with distributional drift away from the SLM's native reasoning distribution. This drift increases the learner's adaptation cost and can outweigh the benefit of improved reasoning logic. To test this mechanism, we introduce \textbf{Style-Aligned Refinement}, which preserves the native trajectory of the SLM while retaining logical repair from the Oracle. This intervention lowers adaptation cost and restores downstream utility. These findings suggest that effective mathematical reasoning distillation should jointly optimize perceived solution quality and learner-data compatibility, rather than relying solely on reward-model scores. The datasets and code are available at https://github.com/Dracoqhl/Quality-Utility-Paradox.
2606.16152.paper_summary_zh: 從強大的推理模型進行知識蒸餾被廣泛用於改善數學推理中的小型語言模型（SLMs），通常假設具有更高獎勵模型分數的痕跡提供了更有用的監督。我們在數學推理蒸餾中識別出一個反直覺的\textbf{質量-效用悖論}。由更強的Oracle精煉或合成的數據根據獎勵模型獲得了更高的感知質量，然而在Qwen2.5、LLaMA-3和DeepSeek系列中，這些數據的表現始終不如SLM自身生成並通過拒絕抽樣選擇的痕跡。我們的分析顯示，Oracle精煉將邏輯修復與SLM的本地推理分佈之間的分佈漂移結合在一起。這種漂移增加了學習者的適應成本，並可能超過改善推理邏輯的好處。為了測試這一機制，我們引入了\textbf{風格對齊精煉}，該方法在保持SLM的本地軌跡的同時保留了來自Oracle的邏輯修復。這一干預降低了適應成本並恢復了下游效用。這些發現表明，有效的數學推理蒸餾應該共同優化感知解決方案質量和學習者-數據兼容性，而不是僅僅依賴於獎勵模型分數。數據集和代碼可在https://github.com/Dracoqhl/Quality-Utility-Paradox獲得。
2606.16152.author: Haolong Qian et.al.
2606.16152.authors: Haolong Qian, Xianliang Yang, Yinuo ma, Lirong Che, Feng Lu, Ye Guo, Lei Song, Jiang Bian, Chun Yuan
